briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon/evening. Welcome to the climate briefing for March 20, 2026. Today, the world is simultaneously confronting the direct impacts of climate change and various efforts to address them.

sections.title: 🔴 Record Heatwave in the Western U.S.: Direct Evidence of Climate Change
sections.content: Despite being March, the Western and Southwestern United States are experiencing a record-breaking heatwave, with scientists warning that such phenomena would be 'virtually impossible' without climate change [28, 29, 32, 35, 37, 41, 45, 54, 55, 56]. Notably, some areas in Arizona recorded 43.3°C, the highest March temperature in U.S. history, demonstrating the severity of 'real-time climate change' [46, 59]. These extreme weather events clearly reveal that climate change is no longer a future threat but a present disaster [30].

sections.title: ⚠️ Spreading Impacts of the Climate Crisis and Social Vulnerability
sections.content: Climate change is profoundly impacting our daily lives and social systems. Domestically, mosquitoes have appeared earlier than usual due to climate change, leading to a nationwide 'Japanese Encephalitis Alert' [2]. Concerns have also been raised that the climate crisis presents a harsher reality for socially vulnerable groups, such as people with disabilities [1]. Internationally, Peru has seen a record outbreak of tropical diseases linked to extreme weather caused by climate change, and the frequent occurrence of allergic diseases like rhinitis is also attributed to climate change [47, 50, 57]. Furthermore, a shocking study finding that human-induced climate change is slowing the Earth's rotation for the first time in 3.6 million years suggests that the climate crisis's impacts are manifesting across the entire Earth system [42].

sections.title: 🌍 Challenges in International Climate Policy and Legal Disputes
sections.content: Policy responses to climate change continue to face difficulties internationally. Several U.S. states, including Vermont and Wisconsin, have joined lawsuits against the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) for abandoning its authority to address climate change, strongly protesting the federal government's passive stance [33, 34, 49, 52]. Meanwhile, climate change bills are consistently stalled in Washington D.C. [40], and even a climate change education bill was scrapped in Vermont, indicating challenges in policy implementation [38]. This situation aligns with the voices of domestic environmental groups that define the climate crisis as a 'present disaster' and urge the implementation of carbon neutrality [8].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Domestic Efforts for Carbon Neutrality and Renewable Energy Transition
sections.content: Amidst the crisis, various domestic efforts to address climate change are accelerating. The South Chungcheong Provincial Council is promoting the development and enhanced support for crops resilient to climate change [4, 5]. Gwangyang City and the Gwangyang Climate and Environment Network plan to operate carbon neutrality practitioner training in 2026 [6, 7]. Gumi City and Gyeongsangbuk-do are actively establishing carbon-neutral industrial complexes, with the Gumi National Industrial Complex being developed as the first model for carbon neutrality [12, 13]. Gimhae City has partnered with local universities to foster young carbon neutrality leaders [17]. Furthermore, Gwangju City and Uijeongbu City are expanding support for renewable energy homes [19, 21, 23], and the Korea Energy Agency is continuing efforts to expand renewable energy by promoting a 'Renewable Energy One Team' initiative and establishing regional headquarters as key hubs [25, 26].

sections.title: 🌱 Setting Global Climate Action Goals and Raising Awareness
sections.content: The international community is focused on setting climate action goals and raising public awareness. Brazil has set an ambitious target to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 59-67% by 2035 compared to 2005 levels [51]. In England, discussions are underway regarding the impacts of a new 'Land Use Framework' on climate, nature, and food [64]. The Caribbean Court of Justice (CCJ) has reiterated its call for the establishment of a climate change compensation fund, amplifying voices for climate justice [58]. Furthermore, the increasing awareness of the climate crisis among younger generations highlights that the participation and interest of future generations are also crucial driving forces [53].

briefing.closing: As confirmed in today's briefing, climate change is having severe global impacts, but simultaneously, various efforts and discussions to overcome it are actively underway. This is a critical time when our sustained attention and action to overcome the climate crisis become even more important. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle barrels west after ripping off roofs and downing trees in far north Queensland
articles.description: Second landfall expected over weekend in NT as Queensland premier says relatively limited damage so far ‘an incredibly good news story’Live updates: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle – latest newsCyclone Narelle path tracking mapHow did global heating fuel Tropical Cyclone Narelle?Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastTropical Cyclone Narelle weakened in intensity on Friday evening after barrelling into far north Queensland as one of the state’s fiercest cyclones in living mem

summary.phenomenon: Tropical Cyclone Narelle made landfall in far north Queensland on Friday evening, causing damage such as tearing off roofs and toppling trees, marking it as one of the most powerful cyclones recorded in the region. Its intensity weakened after landfall.
summary.outlook: A second landfall is anticipated in the Northern Territory (NT) over the weekend, with the Queensland Premier stating that the damage thus far has been relatively limited.
